methods.description: Process for separating proteins in unreduced conditions using 1-dimensional gel electrophoresis (1-DE) SDS-PAGE in a gradient polyacrylamide gel.

steps.description: Proteins were separated in unreduced conditions in 1-dimensional gel electrophoresis (1-DE) SDS-PAGE in a 8%-18% gradient polyacrylamide gel (ExcelGel; GE Healthcare, Uppsala, Sweden) on a flat-bed electrophoretic chamber (Multiphor II; GE Healthcare) at 12°C.
steps.searchable_summary: perform 1-DE SDS-PAGE on proteins using gradient polyacrylamide gel

steps.description: Molecular mass (Mr) markers (GE Healthcare, Little Chalfont, UK) ranging from 14.4 to 94 kDa were used as comparative references.
steps.searchable_summary: use molecular mass markers for reference

steps.description: Separated proteins were then either electrotransferred onto a 0.2 µm cyanogen bromide activated nitrocellulose (NCa) membrane (Optitran ® BA-S 83; Schleicher and Schuell, Dassel, Germany) for immunoblotting assays or stained with Coomassie blue or silver nitrate for protein detection.
steps.searchable_summary: electrotransfer proteins to NCa membrane or stain for detection

methods.description: Process for separating basic proteins using double one-dimensional gel electrophoresis (D1-DE) with prior isoelectric focusing.

steps.description: Cups protein extract was submitted to double one-dimensional gel electrophoresis (D1-DE) with basic proteins (pI 8.5-9.5) previously separated in isoelectric focusing (pH 2-11).
steps.searchable_summary: perform D1-DE on basic proteins after isoelectric focusing

steps.description: Isoelectric point markers (Serva, Heidelberg, Germany) ranging from 4.45 to 9.6 were used as comparative references in first dimension.
steps.searchable_summary: use isoelectric point markers for reference

methods.description: Process for separating proteins using classical two-dimensional gel electrophoresis (2-DE).

steps.description: Cups protein extract was submitted to classical two-dimensional gel electrophoresis (2-DE) according to Shahali et al.
